title: First day at Numble
excerpt: Getting introduced to the company, meeting mentors, and starting with Umbraco.
content:
Today was the first day of my internship at Numble. After arriving at the office, we received an introduction about the company, our mentors, and the webshop project we’ll be working on during the internship. The atmosphere immediately felt welcoming and professional, while still relaxed and approachable.

After setting up our accounts and development tools, we started learning Umbraco, which is completely new to me. We followed tutorials by Paul Seal to get familiar with the CMS and its structure. Even though there was a lot of new information, the onboarding process went smoothly and I’m excited to continue learning and eventually start building the webshop.

title: Learning Umbraco
excerpt: Getting deeper into Umbraco and struggling with extensions.
content:
Most of the day was focused on learning and practicing Umbraco. At first I underestimated how complex the platform actually is. There are many moving parts and concepts that take time to understand.

I spent a lot of time working with extensions and repeatedly ran into small issues that blocked progress. Although it was frustrating at times, I’m slowly starting to understand how everything connects together. I’m trying to solve problems independently before immediately asking for help, since researching solutions myself helps me learn faster.

title: Tech talks and burgers
excerpt: A day of learning, debugging, and interesting discussions about web technologies.
content:
Today was another day focused on learning Umbraco and experimenting with extensions. I spent quite a bit of time debugging small issues that repeatedly broke my implementation, but every mistake helped me understand the system better.

At lunch, the company organized their monthly burger event where everyone gathered together to cook and eat. It created a very relaxed atmosphere and made the office feel much more social.

Later in the day, the team discussed several modern web development topics including Vite, automated testing with Playwright, WCAG accessibility guidelines, and server-side rendering. Even though some concepts were still unfamiliar to me, the discussions were very insightful and showed me how broad modern web development really is.

title: Starting the webshop project
content:
Today we officially started working on the webshop project. During a meeting, we discussed the project requirements and the features that needed to be implemented, such as product pages, filtering, payments, and account management.

This first phase focuses heavily on analysis. We started creating wireframes, user stories, and preparing the database structure. My school mentor also visited the company to discuss the progress of the internship. Overall, the project is starting to feel much more real now that we’ve moved beyond tutorials and into actual planning.

title: Solving concurrency problems
excerpt: Designing a safer approach for stock management.
content:
Today we continued working on the webshop analysis. One interesting challenge was figuring out how to handle concurrency issues, such as two users purchasing the same product at the same time.

Instead of directly modifying stock values, we decided to store purchases as separate database records and calculate stock dynamically from those entries. It was interesting to think about these kinds of real-world problems and how important database design can be for avoiding conflicts.

title: Sprint planning and ticket estimation
excerpt: Preparing the first sprint and organizing project tasks.
content:
Today we held our first sprint meeting. Together we discussed the order in which features and technical setup tasks should be completed.

We split the work into tickets and estimated how long each task would take. It was interesting to see how a professional development team plans projects and prioritizes work. We also discussed infrastructure-related tasks such as Azure setup, database configuration, and project initialization.

title: Azure email service challenges
excerpt: Struggling with domain verification and permissions.
content:
Today I worked on implementing the webshop’s mail service using Azure. Setting everything up turned out to be more complicated than expected because the service required DNS verification on the company domain.

Since I didn’t have permission to modify the necessary DNS records, the setup became a back-and-forth process with my mentors. While waiting for changes to propagate, I temporarily switched to working on the category management dashboard in the backoffice.

title: Building APIs for the backoffice
excerpt: Creating APIs and database tables for category management.
content:
Today I worked on creating APIs for the category overview in the webshop backoffice. Initially the implementation seemed straightforward, but I discovered that Umbraco backoffice integrations required a different API approach than expected.

I also encountered issues while setting up database tables inside Visual Studio, but with help from the documentation and guidance from my mentor I eventually resolved the problems. After several attempts and reviewing the documentation carefully, I finally got the API working correctly.

title: Improving code quality through feedback
excerpt: Learning from pull request reviews and improving development practices.
content:
Today was mostly focused on reviewing pull requests and processing feedback from the team. The reviews were detailed and showed several areas where our code quality could improve.

Although it was sometimes confronting to see mistakes pointed out, the feedback helped me understand better coding practices and improve the overall structure of my work. It showed me how valuable code reviews are in a professional environment.

title: Finally getting member registration working
excerpt: A long debugging session finally pays off.
content:
Today I finally managed to implement user registration using Umbraco Members. At first I tried connecting a separate customer entity to Umbraco Members, but eventually we realized it made more sense to extend the existing member model instead.

I also spent hours debugging an issue where my form data wouldn’t reach the controller. After going through documentation and reviewing my code repeatedly, I eventually found the problem. Solving it felt both satisfying and slightly frustrating because the issue turned out to be something relatively small.

Later in the day, the company organized their monthly burger lunch again, and this time I helped prepare the food together with another intern.

title: Completing account management
excerpt: Login, registration, and account functionality are now finished.
content:
Today progress went smoothly. I finished the account management functionality, including registration and login systems.

It was satisfying to see multiple features finally come together after spending so much time debugging and researching earlier in the project. My next focus will be category properties and improving the webshop structure further.

title: Midterm evaluation
excerpt: Reviewing my internship progress together with school and mentors.
content:
Today was the midterm evaluation of the internship. Together with my mentors and a lecturer from school, we reviewed my progress and discussed different aspects of my performance.

Overall I’m happy with the feedback I received. There are still areas where I can improve, particularly soft skills and communication, but it was motivating to hear that my technical progress is going well.

title: Fixing Razor IntelliSense
content:
Today the company held a quarterly meeting where topics such as efficiency, clients, security vulnerabilities, and internal improvements were discussed.

On the technical side, I finally fixed the IntelliSense issues I had been experiencing in Razor. This problem had been slowing me down significantly because errors only became visible after running the project. Having proper IntelliSense support again immediately improved my workflow and productivity.

title: Project demo and accessibility discussions
excerpt: Presenting our progress and discussing WCAG and security.
content:
Today we attended discussions about WCAG accessibility guidelines and security vulnerabilities related to outdated packages and dependencies.

Later we presented a demo of the webshop to part of the team. While many features were working individually, not everything was fully integrated yet. Even so, the feedback was positive and it was useful to see the project slowly evolving into a complete application.

title: Finishing product variant selection
excerpt: Finally completing one of the more difficult webshop features.
content:
Recently I spent a lot of time working on the product detail page and especially the product variant selection logic. Matching the correct variants and options turned out to be more difficult than expected and led to several messy implementations before I found a cleaner solution.

Today I finally completed the variant selection system. Along the way we also had to redesign part of the shopping cart structure so it could properly support product variants. It was a challenging feature, but seeing it finally work correctly was very rewarding.

title: Company workshop and team event
excerpt: Learning more about internal procedures followed by a creative company activity.
content:
Today we had the second part of the meeting that started on 24/04. This time we went deeper into company guidelines and internal procedures, including topics such as assigning responsibilities, handling incidents, and general workflows within the organization.

At the end of the day we had a company event at Splatter Studio, where we created paintings in groups using different paint splattering techniques. The results were questionable, but it was a fun experience and a good opportunity to interact with colleagues outside of normal work.

Afterwards we all went out for dinner together. Events like these make the workplace feel much more social and enjoyable, and I hope to experience more of them in the future.

title: Integrating the Mollie API
excerpt: Starting payment integration for the webshop.
content:
Today I started integrating the Mollie payment API into the webshop. Initially the task felt intimidating because payment systems seem complex and critical.

Fortunately, the documentation was very clear and well-structured, which made the implementation process much smoother than expected. After understanding the flow and required endpoints, I managed to complete the integration relatively quickly.

title: Temporary new mentor
excerpt: Adjusting to a temporary change in guidance during the internship.
content:
Today we started working with a temporary mentor because our usual mentor went on vacation. Despite the change, the workflow and communication within the team remained mostly the same.

It was interesting to see how smoothly the team adapted and continued working without major disruptions.

title: Project presentations and database improvements
excerpt: Watching final internship presentations and resolving migration issues.
content:
Today two students who were also doing their internship at the company presented their final project and gave a demo of the application they built. Their project turned out very well and it was interesting to see how different teams approached their internship work.

On my side, I once again encountered issues related to database migrations. However, I finally managed to solve the recurring problem where I constantly had to drop and rebuild the database entirely. Fixing this issue should make development much smoother going forward.
